html:
𐤉𐤄𐤅𐤄

THE ANCIENT COVENANT RECORD

Volume Seventeen — Part Four

The Book of Yesha'yahu

YESHA'YAHU

The Book of Isaiah — 1QIsa-a Great Isaiah Scroll

Chapters 42 through 54

The Servant Songs — Koresh My Anointed — The Suffering Servant — Sing O Barren One

PRIMARY MANUSCRIPT AUTHORITIES

1QIsa-a — The Great Isaiah Scroll — Cave 1 Qumran

Ethiopian Orit Ge'ez — Complete Canon

YHWH and The Creator only

COMPARATIVE NOTES AT END OF EVERY CHAPTER

DSS · ORIT GE'EZ · MASORETIC VARIANT · CRITICAL NOTE

YHWH / 𐤉𐤄𐤅𐤄 and The Creator only — no substitute titles

All personal names in original Hebrew form throughout

YESHA'YAHU — CHAPTERS 42 THROUGH 54 — THE SERVANT SONGS AND THE COVENANT OF PEACE

YESHA'YAHU — CHAPTER 42 Behold My Servant — A Bruised Reed He Will Not Break — A New Song — YHWH Goes Out Like a Warrior

1 Behold my servant, whom I uphold, my chosen, in whom my soul delights. I have put my Spirit upon him — he will bring forth justice to the nations.

2 He will not cry aloud or lift up his voice, or make it heard in the street.

3 A bruised reed he will not break, and a faintly burning wick he will not quench — he will faithfully bring forth justice.

4 He will not grow faint or be discouraged till he has established justice in the earth, and the coastlands wait for his Torah.

5 Thus says Creator, YHWH, who created the heavens and stretched them out, who spread out the earth and what comes from it, who gives breath to the people on it and spirit to those who walk in it:

6 I am YHWH — I have called you in righteousness — I will take you by the hand and keep you. I will give you as a covenant for the people, a light for the nations,

7 To open the eyes that are blind, to bring out the prisoners from the dungeon, from the prison those who sit in darkness.

8 I am YHWH — that is my name. My glory I give to no other, nor my praise to carved idols.

9 Behold, the former things have come to pass, and new things I now declare. Before they spring forth I tell you of them.

10 Sing to YHWH a new song, his praise from the end of the earth, you who go down to the sea, and all that fills it, the coastlands and their inhabitants.

11 Let the desert and its cities lift up their voice, the villages that Kedar inhabits. Let the inhabitants of Sela sing for joy, let them shout from the top of the mountains.

12 Let them give glory to YHWH, and declare his praise in the coastlands.

13 YHWH goes out like a warrior — like a man of war he stirs up his zeal. He cries out, he shouts aloud — he shows himself mighty against his foes.

14 For a long time I have held my peace — I have kept still and restrained myself. Now I will cry out like a woman in labor — I will gasp and pant.

15 I will lay waste mountains and hills, and dry up all their vegetation. I will turn the rivers into islands, and dry up the pools.

16 And I will lead the blind in a way that they do not know, in paths that they have not known I will guide them. I will turn the darkness before them into light, the rough places into level ground. These are the things I do, and I do not forsake them.

17 They are turned back and utterly put to shame, who trust in carved idols, who say to metal images, You are our gods.

18 Hear, you deaf, and look, you blind, that you may see.

19 Who is blind but my servant, or deaf as my messenger whom I send? Who is blind as my dedicated one, or blind as the servant of YHWH?

20 He sees many things, but does not observe them. His ears are open, but he does not hear.

21 YHWH was pleased, for his righteousness' sake, to magnify his Torah and make it glorious.

22 But this is a people plundered and looted — they are all of them trapped in holes and hidden in prisons. They have become plunder with none to rescue, spoil with none to say, Restore.

23 Who among you will give ear to this, will attend and listen for the time to come?

24 Who gave up Yaakov to the looter, and Yisra'EL to the plunderers? Was it not YHWH, against whom we have sinned, in whose ways they would not walk, and whose Torah they would not obey?

25 So he poured on him the heat of his anger and the might of battle — it set him on fire all around, but he did not understand — it burned him up, but he did not take it to heart.

COMPARATIVE MANUSCRIPT NOTES

[DSS] 1QIsa-a preserves Chapter 42 in full. The first Servant Song — verses 1-4 — opens the servant theology of the second half of Yesha'yahu. The servant is presented as YHWH's chosen, Spirit-endowed, commissioned to bring covenant justice to the nations without coercion or force: a bruised reed he will not break.

[ORIT GE'EZ] The Orit Ge'ez preserves Chapter 42 in full. Verse 6-7 — I will give you as a covenant for the people, a light for the nations, to open the eyes that are blind, to bring out the prisoners from the dungeon — is read as the covenant universal mission of the servant: the covenant is not merely for Yisra'EL but for all peoples, and the servant is its embodiment.

[MASORETIC VARIANT] 1QIsa-a and the Masoretic Text are in close agreement for Chapter 42. Verse 8 — I am YHWH — that is my name. My glory I give to no other, nor my praise to carved idols — is the covenant declaration of divine exclusivity that frames the servant's mission: the servant carries YHWH's covenant mission precisely because YHWH shares his glory with no other agent.

[CRITICAL NOTE] Verse 1-4 — behold my servant — my chosen, in whom my soul delights — he will bring forth justice to the nations — he will not cry aloud or lift up his voice — a bruised reed he will not break — a faintly burning wick he will not quench. The covenant portrait of the servant is defined entirely by what he will not do: no public display, no coercion, no crushing of the weak. The covenant justice the servant establishes is established through covenant gentleness. Verse 19 — who is blind but my servant? The servant who is exalted in verse 1 is blind in verse 19. The covenant paradox: the one commissioned to open blind eyes is himself blind to covenant reality. The servant identity in Yesha'yahu operates on multiple levels simultaneously.

YESHA'YAHU — CHAPTER 43 Fear Not I Have Redeemed You — You Are Mine — I Am YHWH Your Holy One — Remember Not the Former Things

1 But now thus says YHWH, he who created you, O Yaakov, he who formed you, O Yisra'EL: Fear not, for I have redeemed you — I have called you by name — you are mine.

2 When you pass through the waters, I will be with you, and through the rivers, they shall not overwhelm you. When you walk through fire you shall not be burned, and the flame shall not consume you.

3 For I am YHWH your Creator, the Holy One of Yisra'EL, your Savior. I give Egypt as your ransom, Kush and Seba in exchange for you.

4 Because you are precious in my eyes, and honored, and I love you — I give men in return for you, peoples in exchange for your life.

5 Fear not, for I am with you. I will bring your offspring from the east, and from the west I will gather you.

6 I will say to the north, Give up, and to the south, Do not withhold. Bring my sons from afar and my daughters from the end of the earth,

7 Everyone who is called by my name, whom I created for my glory, whom I formed and made.

8 Bring out the people who are blind, yet have eyes, who are deaf, yet have ears.

9 All the nations gather together, and the peoples assemble. Who among them can declare this, and show us the former things? Let them bring their witnesses to prove them right, and let them hear and say, It is true.

10 You are my witnesses, declares YHWH, and my servant whom I have chosen, that you may know and believe me and understand that I am he. Before me no god was formed, nor shall there be any after me.

11 I, I am YHWH, and besides me there is no savior.

12 I declared and saved and proclaimed, when there was no strange god among you. And you are my witnesses, declares YHWH, and I am Creator.

13 Also henceforth I am he. There is none who can deliver from my hand — I work, and who can turn it back?

14 Thus says YHWH, your Redeemer, the Holy One of Yisra'EL: For your sake I send to Babylon and bring them all down as fugitives, even the Chaldeans, in the ships in which they rejoice.

15 I am YHWH, your Holy One, the Creator of Yisra'EL, your King.

16 Thus says YHWH, who makes a way in the sea, a path in the mighty waters,

17 Who brings forth chariot and horse, army and warrior — they lie down, they cannot rise, they are extinguished, quenched like a wick:

18 Remember not the former things, nor consider the things of old.

19 Behold, I am doing a new thing — now it springs forth, do you not perceive it? I will make a way in the wilderness and rivers in the desert.

20 The wild beasts will honor me, the jackals and the ostriches, for I give water in the wilderness, rivers in the desert, to give drink to my chosen people,

21 The people whom I formed for myself that they might declare my praise.

22 Yet you did not call upon me, O Yaakov — but you have been weary of me, O Yisra'EL.

23 You have not brought me your sheep for burnt offerings, or honored me with your sacrifices. I have not burdened you with offerings, or wearied you with frankincense.

24 You have not bought me sweet cane with money, or satisfied me with the fat of your sacrifices. But you have burdened me with your sins — you have wearied me with your iniquities.

25 I, I am he who blots out your transgressions for my own sake, and I will not remember your sins.

26 Put me in remembrance — let us argue together. Set forth your case, that you may be proved right.

27 Your first father sinned, and your mediators transgressed against me.

28 Therefore I will profane the princes of the sanctuary, and deliver Yaakov to utter destruction and Yisra'EL to reviling.

COMPARATIVE MANUSCRIPT NOTES

[DSS] 1QIsa-a preserves Chapter 43 in full. Verse 10-11 — you are my witnesses, declares YHWH — I, I am YHWH, and besides me there is no savior — is the covenant declaration of absolute monotheism in the second half of Yesha'yahu. The covenant community in exile is designated as YHWH's witnesses to his uniqueness before the nations.

[ORIT GE'EZ] The Orit Ge'ez preserves Chapter 43 in full. Verse 1 — fear not, for I have redeemed you — I have called you by name — you are mine — is read as the covenant foundation of covenant identity: belonging to YHWH is prior to any covenant performance. The redemption and the naming are covenant acts of YHWH, not earned positions.

[MASORETIC VARIANT] 1QIsa-a and the Masoretic Text are in close agreement for Chapter 43. Verse 25 — I, I am he who blots out your transgressions for my own sake, and I will not remember your sins — is the covenant declaration of gratuitous forgiveness: YHWH forgives for his own sake — for the sake of his covenant name and character — not because Yisra'EL has earned it.

[CRITICAL NOTE] Verse 18-19 — remember not the former things, nor consider the things of old. Behold, I am doing a new thing. The covenant theological paradox: the entire prophetic tradition appeals to the Exodus as the covenant precedent for YHWH's action. Now YHWH declares the Exodus insufficient as a model — the new covenant act will be so much greater that the Exodus will recede in comparison. Verse 4 — because you are precious in my eyes, and honored, and I love you. The covenant ground of YHWH's redemption is love and covenant value — not Yisra'EL's performance. The exiled covenant community is declared precious, honored, and loved while in the lowest condition of their national existence.

YESHA'YAHU — CHAPTER 44 Fear Not O Yaakov My Servant — I Will Pour My Spirit — The Folly of Idols — I Have Blotted Out Your Transgressions

1 But now hear, O Yaakov my servant, Yisra'EL whom I have chosen.

2 Thus says YHWH who made you, who formed you from the womb and will help you: Fear not, O Yaakov my servant, Yeshurun whom I have chosen.

3 For I will pour water on the thirsty land, and streams on the dry ground. I will pour my Spirit upon your offspring, and my blessing on your descendants.

4 They shall spring up among the grass like willows by flowing streams.

5 This one will say, I am YHWH's, and another will call on the name of Yaakov, and another will write on his hand, YHWH's, and name himself by the name of Yisra'EL.

6 Thus says YHWH, the King of Yisra'EL and his Redeemer, YHWH of hosts: I am the first and I am the last — besides me there is no god.

7 Who is like me? Let him proclaim it. Let him declare and set it before me, since I appointed an ancient people. Let them declare what is to come, and what will happen.

8 Fear not, nor be afraid. Have I not told you from of old and declared it? And you are my witnesses. Is there a god besides me? There is no Rock — I know not any.

9 All who fashion idols are nothing, and the things they delight in do not profit. Their witnesses neither see nor know, that they may be put to shame.

10 Who fashions a god or casts an idol that is profitable for nothing?

11 Behold, all his companions shall be put to shame, and the craftsmen are only human. Let them all assemble, let them stand forth — they shall be terrified — they shall be put to shame together.

12 The ironsmith takes a cutting tool and works it over the coals. He fashions it with hammers and works it with his strong arm. He becomes hungry, and his strength fails. He drinks no water and is faint.

13 The carpenter stretches a line, he marks it out with a pencil. He shapes it with planes and marks it with a compass. He shapes it into the figure of a man, with the beauty of a man, to dwell in a house.

14 He cuts down cedars, or he chooses a cypress tree or an oak and lets it grow strong among the trees of the forest. He plants a cedar and the rain nourishes it.

15 Then it becomes fuel for a man. He takes a part of it and warms himself — he kindles a fire and bakes bread. Also he makes a god and worships it. He makes it an idol and falls down before it.

16 Half of it he burns in the fire. Over the half he eats meat — he roasts it and is satisfied. Also he warms himself and says, Aha, I am warm, I have seen the fire.

17 And the rest of it he makes into a god, his idol, and falls down to it and worships it. He prays to it and says, Deliver me, for you are my god.

18 They know not, nor do they discern, for he has shut their eyes so that they cannot see, and their hearts, so that they cannot understand.

19 No one considers, nor is there knowledge or discernment to say, Half of it I burned in the fire. I also baked bread on its coals. I roasted meat and have eaten. And shall I make the rest of it an abomination? Shall I fall down before a block of wood?

20 He feeds on ashes — a deluded heart has led him astray. He cannot deliver himself or say, Is there not a lie in my right hand?

21 Remember these things, O Yaakov, and Yisra'EL, for you are my servant. I formed you — you are my servant. O Yisra'EL, you will not be forgotten by me.

22 I have blotted out your transgressions like a cloud and your sins like mist. Return to me, for I have redeemed you.

23 Sing, O heavens, for YHWH has done it. Shout, O depths of the earth. Break forth into singing, O mountains, O forest, and every tree in it. For YHWH has redeemed Yaakov, and will be glorified in Yisra'EL.

24 Thus says YHWH, your Redeemer, who formed you from the womb: I am YHWH, who made all things, who alone stretched out the heavens, who spread out the earth by myself —

25 Who frustrates the signs of liars and makes fools of diviners, who turns wise men back and makes their knowledge foolish,

26 Who confirms the word of his servant and fulfills the counsel of his messengers, who says of Yerushalayim, She shall be inhabited, and of the cities of Yehudah, They shall be built, and I will raise up their ruins —

27 Who says to the deep, Be dry — I will dry up your rivers —

28 Who says of Koresh, He is my shepherd, and he shall fulfill all my purpose. He shall say of Yerushalayim, She shall be built, and of the temple, Your foundation shall be laid.

COMPARATIVE MANUSCRIPT NOTES

[DSS] 1QIsa-a preserves Chapter 44 in full. Verse 28 — who says of Koresh, he is my shepherd — is the first explicit naming of Koresh (Cyrus the Great) in Yesha'yahu. 1QIsa-a preserves the name clearly. The covenant declaration of a pagan Persian king as YHWH's shepherd is one of the most startling covenant designations in the prophetic corpus.

[ORIT GE'EZ] The Orit Ge'ez preserves Chapter 44 in full. Verses 12-20 — the extended satire of idol making — is read as the covenant demonstration of the absurdity of idolatry through careful forensic observation: the same tree provides firewood and deity. The covenant logic is unanswerable.

[MASORETIC VARIANT] 1QIsa-a and the Masoretic Text are in close agreement for Chapter 44. Verse 22 — I have blotted out your transgressions like a cloud and your sins like mist. Return to me, for I have redeemed you. The covenant sequence: blotting out precedes the call to return. YHWH declares the forgiveness before calling for the response.

[CRITICAL NOTE] Verse 28 — who says of Koresh, he is my shepherd, and he shall fulfill all my purpose. The covenant designation of Koresh as YHWH's shepherd — the same title used for Dawid and for YHWH himself — is the covenant declaration that YHWH's purposes are not limited to covenant people as his instruments. A Persian emperor who does not know YHWH — verse 45:4 — is called and equipped for a covenant purpose. The covenant sovereignty of YHWH over all nations means he can call and use any human instrument for his covenant purposes.

YESHA'YAHU — CHAPTER 45 Thus Says YHWH to His Anointed, to Koresh — I Am YHWH and There Is No Other — Turn to Me and Be Saved

1 Thus says YHWH to his anointed, to Koresh, whose right hand I have grasped, to subdue nations before him and to loose the belts of kings, to open doors before him that gates may not be closed:

2 I will go before you and level the exalted places. I will break in pieces the doors of bronze and cut through the bars of iron.

3 I will give you the treasures of darkness and the hoards in secret places, that you may know that it is I, YHWH, the Creator of Yisra'EL, who call you by your name.

4 For the sake of my servant Yaakov, and Yisra'EL my chosen, I call you by your name. I name you, though you do not know me.

5 I am YHWH, and there is no other. Besides me there is no god. I equip you, though you do not know me,

6 That people may know, from the rising of the sun and from the west, that there is none besides me. I am YHWH, and there is no other.

7 I form light and create darkness. I make well-being and create calamity. I am YHWH, who does all these things.

8 Shower, O heavens, from above, and let the clouds rain down righteousness. Let the earth open, that salvation and righteousness may bear fruit. Let the earth cause them both to sprout. I am YHWH who has created it.

9 Woe to him who strives with him who formed him, a pot among earthen pots. Does the clay say to him who forms it, What are you making? or, Your work has no handles?

10 Woe to him who says to a father, What are you begetting? or to a woman, What are you in labor with?

11 Thus says YHWH, the Holy One of Yisra'EL, and the one who formed him: Ask me of things to come — will you command me concerning my children and the work of my hands?

12 I made the earth and created man on it — it was my hands that stretched out the heavens, and I commanded all their host.

13 I have stirred him up in righteousness, and I will make all his ways level. He shall build my city and set my exiles free, not for price or reward, says YHWH of hosts.

14 Thus says YHWH: The wealth of Egypt and the merchandise of Kush, and the Sabeans, men of stature, shall come over to you and be yours — they shall follow you. They shall come over in chains and bow down to you. They will plead with you, saying: Surely Creator is in you, and there is no other, no god besides him.

15 Truly, you are a Creator who hides himself, O Creator of Yisra'EL, the Savior.

16 All of them are put to shame and confounded — the makers of idols go in confusion together.

17 But Yisra'EL is saved by YHWH with everlasting salvation. You shall not be put to shame or confounded to all eternity.

18 For thus says YHWH, who created the heavens — he is Creator — who formed the earth and made it — he established it — he did not create it empty, he formed it to be inhabited: I am YHWH, and there is no other.

19 I did not speak in secret, in a land of darkness — I did not say to the offspring of Yaakov, Seek me in vain. I YHWH speak the truth — I declare what is right.

20 Assemble yourselves and come — draw near together, you survivors of the nations. They have no knowledge who carry about their wooden idols, and keep on praying to a god that cannot save.

21 Declare and present your case — let them take counsel together. Who told this long ago? Who declared it of old? Was it not I, YHWH? And there is no other god besides me, a righteous Creator and a Savior — there is none besides me.

22 Turn to me and be saved, all the ends of the earth. For I am Creator, and there is no other.

23 By myself I have sworn — from my mouth has gone out in righteousness a word that shall not return: To me every knee shall bow, every tongue shall swear allegiance.

24 Only in YHWH, it shall be said of me, are righteousness and strength. To him shall come and be ashamed all who were incensed against him.

25 In YHWH all the offspring of Yisra'EL shall be justified and shall glory.

COMPARATIVE MANUSCRIPT NOTES

[DSS] 1QIsa-a preserves Chapter 45 in full. Verse 1 — thus says YHWH to his anointed, to Koresh — is the only instance in the Hebrew canon where a non-Israelite is designated mashiakh — anointed. 1QIsa-a preserves the term clearly. The covenant designation of Koresh as YHWH's anointed establishes that the anointing is a functional covenant designation, not an ethnic or religious qualification.

[ORIT GE'EZ] The Orit Ge'ez preserves Chapter 45 in full. Verse 22-23 — turn to me and be saved, all the ends of the earth — to me every knee shall bow, every tongue shall swear allegiance — is read as the covenant universalism that defines the second half of Yesha'yahu: YHWH's covenant salvation is offered to all the ends of the earth simultaneously.

[MASORETIC VARIANT] 1QIsa-a and the Masoretic Text are in close agreement for Chapter 45. Verse 7 — I form light and create darkness, I make well-being and create calamity — is the covenant monotheistic declaration that YHWH is the sovereign origin of all that occurs. There is no dualistic counter-deity of darkness — only YHWH who creates and governs all.

[CRITICAL NOTE] Verse 4-5 — for the sake of my servant Yaakov — I name you, though you do not know me. I am YHWH and there is no other — I equip you, though you do not know me. The covenant paradox of verse 4-5: Koresh is named, equipped, and used by YHWH for covenant purposes while being entirely ignorant of YHWH. The covenant sovereignty operates without requiring the instrument's covenant knowledge. The covenant purpose — the release of the exiles — is achieved through a Persian king who worships Ahura Mazda. Verse 15 — truly, you are a Creator who hides himself. The covenant community's recognition that YHWH's ways are hidden — operating through a Persian king, using exile as covenant discipline — is the covenant confession that YHWH's covenant purposes exceed visible covenant categories.

YESHA'YAHU — CHAPTER 46 Bel Bows Down — Nebo Stoops — They Cannot Save the Burden — I Have Made and I Will Carry You

1 Bel bows down — Nebo stoops. Their idols are on beasts and livestock. These things you carry are borne as burdens on weary beasts.

2 They stoop — they bow down together. They cannot save the burden, but they themselves go into captivity.

3 Listen to me, O house of Yaakov, all the remnant of the house of Yisra'EL, who have been borne by me from before your birth, carried from the womb.

4 Even to your old age I am he, and to gray hairs I will carry you. I have made, and I will bear — I will carry and will save.

5 To whom will you liken me and make me equal, and compare me, that we may be alike?

6 Those who lavish gold from the purse, and weigh out silver in the scales, hire a goldsmith, and he makes it into a god. Then they fall down and worship.

7 They lift it to their shoulders, they carry it, they set it in its place, and it stands there. It cannot move from its place. If one cries to it, it does not answer or save him from his trouble.

8 Remember this and stand firm — recall it to mind, you transgressors.

9 Remember the former things of old — for I am Creator, and there is no other. I am Creator, and there is none like me,

10 Declaring the end from the beginning and from ancient times things not yet done, saying, My counsel shall stand, and I will accomplish all my purpose,

11 Calling a bird of prey from the east, the man of my counsel from a far country. I have spoken, and I will bring it to pass — I have purposed, and I will do it.

12 Listen to me, you stubborn of heart, you who are far from righteousness.

13 I bring near my righteousness — it is not far off, and my salvation will not delay. I will put salvation in Tziyon, for Yisra'EL my glory.

COMPARATIVE MANUSCRIPT NOTES

[DSS] 1QIsa-a preserves Chapter 46 in full. The covenant contrast between the Babylonian gods — Bel and Nebo, carried on beasts into exile — and YHWH who carries his people from birth to old age — is the covenant theological center of the chapter. The idols must be carried; YHWH carries. Verse 4 — I have made, and I will bear — I will carry and will save.

[ORIT GE'EZ] The Orit Ge'ez preserves Chapter 46 in full. Verse 3-4 — who have been borne by me from before your birth, carried from the womb — even to your old age I am he, and to gray hairs I will carry you — is read as the covenant declaration of YHWH's lifelong covenant faithfulness from womb to grave.

[MASORETIC VARIANT] 1QIsa-a and the Masoretic Text are in close agreement for Chapter 46. Verse 10 — declaring the end from the beginning and from ancient times things not yet done — is the covenant declaration of YHWH's unique prophetic capacity: he alone knows the end from the beginning because he governs both.

[CRITICAL NOTE] Verse 1-2 versus verse 3-4 — the Babylonian gods must be carried into exile as burdens on weary animals. YHWH carries his people. The covenant contrast is physical and sustained: the idols are dead weight requiring transportation; YHWH is the living carrier who has borne his people from conception. The covenant question embedded in the contrast: why would you exchange a Creator who carries you for gods you must carry? The exchange is irrational by every measure. Yet Yisra'EL did it — verse 8 — you transgressors.

YESHA'YAHU — CHAPTER 47 Come Down O Virgin Daughter of Babylon — You Said I Shall Be Mistress Forever — Your Sorceries Cannot Save You

1 Come down and sit in the dust, O virgin daughter of Babylon. Sit on the ground without a throne, O daughter of the Chaldeans. For you shall no more be called tender and delicate.

2 Take the millstones and grind flour — put off your veil, strip off your robe, uncover your legs, pass through the rivers.

3 Your nakedness shall be uncovered, and your disgrace shall be seen. I will take vengeance, and I will spare no one.

4 Our Redeemer — YHWH of hosts is his name — is the Holy One of Yisra'EL.

5 Sit in silence, and go into darkness, O daughter of the Chaldeans, for you shall no more be called the mistress of kingdoms.

6 I was angry with my people — I profaned my heritage — I gave them into your hand. You showed them no mercy. On the aged you made your yoke exceedingly heavy.

7 You said, I shall be mistress forever, so that you did not lay these things to heart or remember their end.

8 Now therefore hear this, you lover of pleasures, who sit securely, who say in your heart, I am, and there is no one besides me. I shall not sit as a widow or know the loss of children.

9 These two things shall come to you in a moment, in one day — the loss of children and widowhood shall come upon you in full measure, in spite of your many sorceries and the great power of your enchantments.

10 You felt secure in your wickedness — you said, No one sees me. Your wisdom and your knowledge led you astray, and you said in your heart, I am, and there is no one besides me.

11 But evil shall come upon you, which you will not know how to charm away. Disaster shall fall upon you, for which you will not be able to atone. And ruin shall come upon you suddenly, of which you know nothing.

12 Stand fast in your enchantments and your many sorceries, with which you have labored from your youth — perhaps you may be able to succeed, perhaps you may inspire terror.

13 You are wearied with your many counsels. Let them stand forth and save you, those who divide the heavens, who gaze at the stars, who at the new moons make known what shall come upon you.

14 Behold, they are like stubble — the fire consumes them. They cannot deliver themselves from the power of the flame. No coal for warming oneself is this, no fire to sit before.

15 Such to you are those with whom you have labored, who have done business with you from your youth. They all wander about in their own paths — there is no one to save you.

COMPARATIVE MANUSCRIPT NOTES

[DSS] 1QIsa-a preserves Chapter 47 in full. The oracle against Babylon is addressed as a woman — virgin daughter of Babylon — in covenant poetic parallel to the daughter of Tziyon imagery used for Yerushalayim. The reversal is complete: Babylon who sat as queen will sit in the dust; Tziyon who sat in the dust will be raised to glory.

[ORIT GE'EZ] The Orit Ge'ez preserves Chapter 47 in full. Verse 6 — I was angry with my people — I profaned my heritage — I gave them into your hand — you showed them no mercy. The covenant diagnosis of Babylon's guilt: YHWH used Babylon as a covenant instrument of discipline, but Babylon exceeded the covenant commission by showing no mercy. The instrument is judged for going beyond its mandate.

[MASORETIC VARIANT] 1QIsa-a and the Masoretic Text are in close agreement for Chapter 47. Verse 8 — I am, and there is no one besides me — is the exact covenant formula YHWH uses of himself in Chapter 45:5-6. Babylon blasphemously appropriates the divine self-designation. This presumption — I am — is the covenant ground of its judgment.

[CRITICAL NOTE] Verse 7-8 — you said, I shall be mistress forever — you said in your heart, I am, and there is no one besides me. The covenant sin of Babylon is not merely oppression but the claim to divine permanence and uniqueness. I am — the divine covenant name — is appropriated by the empire. Babylon's collapse is the covenant demonstration that no human power can sustain the divine self-designation. Verse 10 — your wisdom and your knowledge led you astray. The covenant warning against the intellectualization of power: Babylon's advanced knowledge of astronomy and divination became the source of its fatal covenant overconfidence.

YESHA'YAHU — CHAPTER 48 I Declared Former Things From of Old — New Things I Now Tell You — For My Own Sake I Defer My Anger — Go Out From Babylon

1 Hear this, O house of Yaakov, who are called by the name of Yisra'EL, and who came from the waters of Yehudah, who swear by the name of YHWH and confess the Creator of Yisra'EL, but not in truth or right.

2 For they call themselves after the holy city, and stay themselves on the Creator of Yisra'EL — YHWH of hosts is his name.

3 The former things I declared of old — they went out from my mouth, and I announced them. Then suddenly I did them, and they came to pass.

4 Because I know that you are obstinate, and your neck is an iron sinew and your forehead brass,

5 I declared them to you from of old, before they came to pass I announced them to you, lest you should say, My idol did them, my carved image and my metal image commanded them.

6 You have heard — now see all this, and will you not declare it? From this time forth I announce to you new things, hidden things that you have not known.

7 They are created now, not long ago, and before today you have never heard of them, lest you should say, Behold, I knew them.

8 You have never heard, you have never known, from of old your ear has not been opened. For I knew that you would surely deal treacherously, and that from before birth you were called a rebel.

9 For my name's sake I defer my anger — for the sake of my praise I restrain it for you, that I may not cut you off.

10 Behold, I have refined you, but not as silver. I have tried you in the furnace of affliction.

11 For my own sake, for my own sake, I do it — for how should my name be profaned? My glory I will not give to another.

12 Listen to me, O Yaakov, and Yisra'EL, whom I called. I am he — I am the first, and I am the last.

13 My hand laid the foundation of the earth, and my right hand spread out the heavens. When I call to them, they stand forth together.

14 Assemble, all of you, and listen. Who among them has declared these things? YHWH loves him — he shall perform his purpose on Babylon, and his arm shall be against the Chaldeans.

15 I, even I, have spoken and called him. I have brought him, and he will prosper in his way.

16 Draw near to me, hear this: from the beginning I have not spoken in secret. From the time it came to be I have been there. And now the Lord YHWH has sent me, and his Spirit.

17 Thus says YHWH, your Redeemer, the Holy One of Yisra'EL: I am YHWH your Creator, who teaches you to profit, who leads you in the way you should go.

18 Oh that you had paid attention to my commandments. Then your peace would have been like a river, and your righteousness like the waves of the sea.

19 Your offspring would have been like the sand, and your descendants like its grains — their name would never be cut off or destroyed from before me.

20 Go out from Babylon — flee from Chaldea. Declare this with a shout of joy — proclaim it — send it out to the end of the earth. Say, YHWH has redeemed his servant Yaakov.

21 They did not thirst when he led them through the deserts — he made water flow for them from the rock. He split the rock and the water gushed out.

22 There is no peace, says YHWH, for the wicked.

COMPARATIVE MANUSCRIPT NOTES

[DSS] 1QIsa-a preserves Chapter 48 in full. Verse 16 — from the beginning I have not spoken in secret — from the time it came to be I have been there — and now the Lord YHWH has sent me, and his Spirit — is one of the most complex verses in the servant theology. A speaker distinct from YHWH declares that YHWH and his Spirit have sent him.

[ORIT GE'EZ] The Orit Ge'ez preserves Chapter 48 in full. Verse 17-18 — I am YHWH your Creator, who teaches you to profit, who leads you in the way you should go — oh that you had paid attention to my commandments — is read as the covenant declaration of YHWH's pedagogical role: the covenant is not merely legal obligation but divine instruction for covenant flourishing.

[MASORETIC VARIANT] 1QIsa-a and the Masoretic Text are in close agreement for Chapter 48. Verse 10 — I have refined you, but not as silver. I have tried you in the furnace of affliction. The covenant refinement of the exile is not the silver-refinement of elimination of dross but something more severe: the furnace of affliction without the silver standard. The exile exceeded normal covenant discipline.

[CRITICAL NOTE] Verse 9-11 — for my name's sake I defer my anger — for my own sake, for my own sake, I do it — for how should my name be profaned? The double covenant declaration: YHWH's restraint of judgment is entirely for his own covenant name's sake, not for Yisra'EL's merit. And the redemption from Babylon is equally for his own sake. The covenant people are saved not because they deserve it but because their destruction would bring reproach on YHWH's covenant name. The covenant logic is entirely theocentric. Verse 22 — there is no peace for the wicked. The covenant closure declaration that will appear again at the end of Chapter 57 and in the final contrast of Chapter 66.

YESHA'YAHU — CHAPTER 49 The Second Servant Song — I Will Make You a Light to the Nations — Tziyon Says YHWH Has Forsaken Me — Can a Mother Forget Her Nursing Child?

1 Listen to me, O coastlands, and give attention, you peoples from afar. YHWH called me from the womb, from the body of my mother he named my name.

2 He made my mouth like a sharp sword — in the shadow of his hand he hid me. He made me a polished arrow — in his quiver he hid me away.

3 And he said to me, You are my servant, Yisra'EL, in whom I will be glorified.

4 But I said, I have labored in vain — I have spent my strength for nothing and vanity. Yet surely my right is with YHWH, and my recompense with my Creator.

5 And now YHWH says, he who formed me from the womb to be his servant, to bring Yaakov back to him, and that Yisra'EL might be gathered to him — for I am honored in the eyes of YHWH, and my Creator has become my strength —

6 He says: It is too light a thing that you should be my servant to raise up the tribes of Yaakov and to bring back the preserved of Yisra'EL. I will make you as a light for the nations, that my salvation may reach to the end of the earth.

7 Thus says YHWH, the Redeemer of Yisra'EL and his Holy One, to one deeply despised, abhorred by the nation, the servant of rulers: Kings shall see and arise, princes, and they shall prostrate themselves, because of YHWH, who is faithful, the Holy One of Yisra'EL, who has chosen you.

8 Thus says YHWH: In a time of favor I have answered you, in a day of salvation I have helped you. I will keep you and give you as a covenant for the people, to establish the land, to apportion the desolate heritages,

9 Saying to the prisoners, Come out, to those who are in darkness, Appear. They shall feed along the ways — on all bare heights shall be their pasture.

10 They shall not hunger or thirst, neither scorching wind nor sun shall strike them — for he who has pity on them will lead them, and by springs of water will guide them.

11 And I will make all my mountains a road, and my highways shall be raised up.

12 Behold, these shall come from afar, and behold, these from the north and from the west, and these from the land of Sinim.

13 Sing for joy, O heavens, and exult, O earth. Break forth, O mountains, into singing. For YHWH has comforted his people and will have compassion on his afflicted.

14 But Tziyon said, YHWH has forsaken me — my Lord has forgotten me.

15 Can a woman forget her nursing child, that she should have no compassion on the son of her womb? Even these may forget, yet I will not forget you.

16 Behold, I have engraved you on the palms of my hands — your walls are continually before me.

17 Your builders come quickly — your destroyers and those who laid you waste go out from you.

18 Lift up your eyes around and see — they all gather, they come to you. As I live, declares YHWH, you shall put them all on as an ornament — you shall bind them on as a bride does.

19 Surely your waste and your desolate places and your devastated land — surely now you will be too narrow for your inhabitants, and those who swallowed you up will be far away.

20 The children of your bereavement will yet say in your ears: The place is too narrow for me — make room for me to dwell in.

21 Then you will say in your heart: Who has borne me these? I was bereaved and barren, exiled and put away — but who has brought up these? Behold, I was left alone — from where have these come?

22 Thus says the Lord YHWH: Behold, I will lift up my hand to the nations and raise my signal to the peoples. And they shall bring your sons in their arms and your daughters shall be carried on their shoulders.

23 Kings shall be your foster fathers, and their queens your nursing mothers. With their faces to the ground they shall bow down to you, and lick the dust of your feet. Then you will know that I am YHWH — those who wait for me shall not be put to shame.

24 Can the prey be taken from the mighty, or the captives of a tyrant be rescued?

25 For thus says YHWH: Even the captives of the mighty shall be taken, and the prey of the tyrant be rescued — for I will contend with those who contend with you, and I will save your children.

26 I will make your oppressors eat their own flesh, and they shall be drunk with their own blood as with wine. Then all flesh shall know that I am YHWH your Savior, and your Redeemer, the Mighty One of Yaakov.

COMPARATIVE MANUSCRIPT NOTES

[DSS] 1QIsa-a preserves Chapter 49 in full. The second Servant Song — verses 1-6 — expands the servant's covenant commission beyond Yisra'EL: it is too light a thing to raise up the tribes of Yaakov — I will make you a light for the nations, that my salvation may reach to the end of the earth. The covenant mission of the servant is universal in scope.

[ORIT GE'EZ] The Orit Ge'ez preserves Chapter 49 in full. Verse 15-16 — can a woman forget her nursing child? Even these may forget, yet I will not forget you. Behold, I have engraved you on the palms of my hands — is read as the covenant declaration of YHWH's maternal covenant memory: YHWH's covenant faithfulness exceeds the strongest human bond.

[MASORETIC VARIANT] 1QIsa-a and the Masoretic Text are in close agreement for Chapter 49. Verse 14 — Tziyon said, YHWH has forsaken me, my Lord has forgotten me — is the covenant lament of the exiled community. YHWH's response in verse 15-16 is the covenant counter-declaration: the engraving of Tziyon's name on YHWH's palms.

[CRITICAL NOTE] Verse 6 — it is too light a thing that you should be my servant to raise up the tribes of Yaakov — I will make you a light for the nations. The covenant commission is expanded not contracted in response to the servant's apparent failure — verse 4, I have labored in vain. The covenant response to the servant's sense of futility is not consolation but an enlarged commission. Verse 16 — I have engraved you on the palms of my hands. The covenant image: YHWH's hands — the hands that stretched out the heavens and laid the foundations of the earth — carry the name of Tziyon permanently engraved.

YESHA'YAHU — CHAPTER 50 The Third Servant Song — YHWH Has Not Divorced — The Servant Sets His Face Like Flint — Who Will Contend With Me?

1 Thus says YHWH: Where is your mother's certificate of divorce, with which I sent her away? Or which of my creditors is it to whom I have sold you? Behold, for your iniquities you were sold, and for your transgressions your mother was sent away.

2 Why, when I came, was there no man? Why, when I called, was there no one to answer? Is my hand shortened, that it cannot redeem? Or have I no power to deliver? Behold, by my rebuke I dry up the sea. I make the rivers a desert. Their fish stink for lack of water and die of thirst.

3 I clothe the heavens with blackness and make sackcloth their covering.

4 The Lord YHWH has given me the tongue of those who are taught, that I may know how to sustain with a word him who is weary. Morning by morning he awakens — he awakens my ear to hear as those who are taught.

5 The Lord YHWH has opened my ear, and I was not rebellious — I turned not backward.

6 I gave my back to those who strike, and my cheeks to those who pull out the beard. I hid not my face from disgrace and spitting.

7 But the Lord YHWH helps me — therefore I have not been disgraced. Therefore I have set my face like a flint, and I know that I shall not be put to shame.

8 He who vindicates me is near. Who will contend with me? Let us stand up together. Who is my adversary? Let him come near to me.

9 Behold, the Lord YHWH helps me. Who will declare me guilty? Behold, all of them will wear out like a garment — the moth will eat them up.

10 Who among you fears YHWH and obeys the voice of his servant? Let him who walks in darkness and has no light trust in the name of YHWH and rely on his Creator.

11 Behold, all you who kindle a fire, who equip yourselves with burning torches. Walk by the light of your fire, and by the torches that you have kindled. This you have from my hand: you shall lie down in torment.

COMPARATIVE MANUSCRIPT NOTES

[DSS] 1QIsa-a preserves Chapter 50 in full. The third Servant Song — verses 4-9 — introduces the covenant suffering of the servant: he gave his back to those who strike, his cheeks to those who pull out the beard, and his face was not hidden from disgrace and spitting. The covenant servant absorbs the violence of covenant rejection without resistance.

[ORIT GE'EZ] The Orit Ge'ez preserves Chapter 50 in full. Verse 4 — the Lord YHWH has given me the tongue of those who are taught, that I may know how to sustain with a word him who is weary — is read as the covenant pedagogical gift: the servant's speech is not self-generated but taught by YHWH morning by morning.

[MASORETIC VARIANT] 1QIsa-a and the Masoretic Text are in close agreement for Chapter 50. Verse 7 — I have set my face like a flint, and I know that I shall not be put to shame. The covenant determination of the servant facing opposition: not the hardness of stubbornness but the covenant confidence that YHWH's vindication is certain.

[CRITICAL NOTE] Verse 1 — where is your mother's certificate of divorce? The covenant legal challenge: Yisra'EL assumed YHWH had divorced and sold them — the exile felt like permanent covenant abandonment. YHWH's covenant counter-question: produce the divorce certificate. No such document exists. The exile was covenant discipline, not covenant divorce. The covenant relationship is intact. Verse 6-7 — I gave my back to those who strike — I hid not my face from disgrace and spitting — but YHWH helps me — therefore I have not been disgraced. The servant's covenant posture: non-resistance to violence combined with absolute covenant confidence in YHWH's vindication. The shame inflicted externally does not produce internal shame because YHWH's verdict supersedes human verdict.

YESHA'YAHU — CHAPTER 51 Look to Avraham Your Father — My Salvation Will Be Forever — Awake Awake Put On Strength O Arm of YHWH — You Have Drunk the Cup

1 Listen to me, you who pursue righteousness, you who seek YHWH: look to the rock from which you were hewn, and to the quarry from which you were dug.

2 Look to Avraham your father and to Sarah who bore you — for he was but one when I called him, that I might bless him and multiply him.

3 For YHWH has comforted Tziyon — he has comforted all her waste places and has made her wilderness like Eden, her desert like the garden of YHWH. Joy and gladness will be found in her, thanksgiving and the voice of song.

4 Give attention to me, my people, and give ear to me, my nation — for a Torah will go out from me, and I will set my justice for a light to the peoples.

5 My righteousness draws near, my salvation has gone out, and my arms will judge the peoples. The coastlands hope for me, and for my arm they wait.

6 Lift up your eyes to the heavens, and look at the earth beneath — for the heavens vanish like smoke, the earth will wear out like a garment, and they who dwell in it will die in like manner. But my salvation will be forever, and my righteousness will never be dismayed.

7 Listen to me, you who know righteousness, the people in whose heart is my Torah. Fear not the reproach of man, nor be dismayed at their revilings.

8 For the moth will eat them up like a garment, and the worm will eat them like wool. But my righteousness will be forever, and my salvation to all generations.

9 Awake, awake, put on strength, O arm of YHWH. Awake, as in days of old, the generations of long ago. Was it not you who cut Rahav in pieces, who pierced the dragon?

10 Was it not you who dried up the sea, the waters of the great deep, who made the depths of the sea a way for the redeemed to pass over?

11 And the ransomed of YHWH shall return and come to Tziyon with singing. Everlasting joy shall be upon their heads — they shall obtain gladness and joy, and sorrow and sighing shall flee away.

12 I, I am he who comforts you. Who are you that you are afraid of man who dies, of the son of man who is made like grass,

13 And have forgotten YHWH, your Maker, who stretched out the heavens and laid the foundations of the earth, and you fear continually all the day because of the wrath of the oppressor, when he sets himself to destroy? And where is the wrath of the oppressor?

14 He who is bowed down shall speedily be released — he shall not die and go down to the pit, neither shall his bread be lacking.

15 I am YHWH your Creator, who stirs up the sea so that its waves roar — YHWH of hosts is his name.

16 And I have put my words in your mouth and covered you in the shadow of my hand, establishing the heavens and laying the foundations of the earth, and saying to Tziyon, You are my people.

17 Wake yourself, wake yourself, stand up, O Yerushalayim, you who have drunk from the hand of YHWH the cup of his wrath, who have drunk to the dregs the bowl, the cup of staggering.

18 There is none to guide her among all the sons she has borne — there is none to take her by the hand among all the sons she has brought up.

19 These two things have happened to you — who will console you? Devastation and destruction, famine and sword — who will comfort you?

20 Your sons have fainted — they lie at the head of every street like an antelope in a net. They are full of the wrath of YHWH, the rebuke of your Creator.

21 Therefore hear this, you who are afflicted, who are drunk, but not with wine.

22 Thus says your Lord, YHWH, your Creator who pleads the cause of his people: Behold, I have taken from your hand the cup of staggering — the bowl of my wrath you shall drink no more.

23 And I will put it into the hand of your tormentors, who have said to you, Bow down, that we may pass over. And you have made your back like the ground and like the street for them to pass over.

COMPARATIVE MANUSCRIPT NOTES

[DSS] 1QIsa-a preserves Chapter 51 in full. Verse 9-10 — awake, O arm of YHWH — was it not you who cut Rahav in pieces, who pierced the dragon? Was it not you who dried up the sea? — is the covenant appeal to the Exodus as the covenant precedent for the new redemption. The same arm that slew the chaos dragon and parted the Red Sea will act again.

[ORIT GE'EZ] The Orit Ge'ez preserves Chapter 51 in full. Verse 2 — look to Avraham your father — for he was but one when I called him, that I might bless him and multiply him — is read as the covenant foundation of the remnant theology: YHWH began with one and multiplied. The exiles are that same one, and YHWH will multiply again.

[MASORETIC VARIANT] 1QIsa-a and the Masoretic Text are in close agreement for Chapter 51. Verse 11 — the ransomed of YHWH shall return and come to Tziyon with singing — exactly repeating the closing verse of Chapter 35. The covenant refrain of return and joy brackets the entire second half of Yesha'yahu's restoration promises.

[CRITICAL NOTE] Verse 17-22 — wake yourself, O Yerushalayim, you who have drunk the cup of YHWH's wrath to the dregs — I have taken from your hand the cup of staggering — you shall drink no more — and I will put it into the hand of your tormentors. The covenant cup of judgment is transferred. Yerushalayim has drunk the full measure of covenant judgment. The cup is now given to the oppressors. The same covenant logic operates throughout: the instrument of judgment is itself judged for its excess.

THE FOURTH SERVANT SONG — CHAPTERS 52 THROUGH 54 — THE SUFFERING AND GLORY OF THE SERVANT

YESHA'YAHU — CHAPTER 52 Awake Awake Put On Your Strength O Tziyon — How Beautiful Are the Feet — The Beginning of the Fourth Servant Song

1 Awake, awake, put on your strength, O Tziyon. Put on your beautiful garments, O Yerushalayim, the holy city. For there shall no more come into you the uncircumcised and the unclean.

2 Shake yourself from the dust and arise, O captive Yerushalayim. Loose the bonds from your neck, O captive daughter of Tziyon.

3 For thus says YHWH: You were sold for nothing, and you shall be redeemed without money.

4 For thus says the Lord YHWH: My people went down at the first into Egypt to sojourn there, and the Assyrian oppressed them for nothing.

5 Now therefore what have I here, declares YHWH, seeing that my people are taken away for nothing? Their rulers wail, declares YHWH, and continually all the day my name is despised.

6 Therefore my people shall know my name. Therefore in that day they shall know that it is I who speak — here I am.

7 How beautiful upon the mountains are the feet of him who brings good news, who publishes peace, who brings good news of happiness, who publishes salvation, who says to Tziyon, Your Creator reigns.

8 The voice of your watchmen — they lift up their voice — together they sing for joy. For eye to eye they see the return of YHWH to Tziyon.

9 Break forth together into singing, you waste places of Yerushalayim, for YHWH has comforted his people — he has redeemed Yerushalayim.

10 YHWH has bared his holy arm before the eyes of all the nations, and all the ends of the earth shall see the salvation of our Creator.

11 Depart, depart, go out from there — touch no unclean thing. Go out from the midst of her — purify yourselves, you who bear the vessels of YHWH.

12 For you shall not go out in haste, and you shall not go in flight, for YHWH will go before you, and the Creator of Yisra'EL will be your rear guard.

13 Behold, my servant shall act wisely — he shall be high and lifted up, and shall be exalted.

14 As many were astonished at you — his appearance was so marred, beyond human semblance, and his form beyond that of the children of mankind —

15 So shall he sprinkle many nations. Kings shall shut their mouths because of him — for that which has not been told them they see, and that which they have not heard they understand.

COMPARATIVE MANUSCRIPT NOTES

[DSS] 1QIsa-a preserves Chapter 52 in full. Verses 13-15 — behold, my servant shall act wisely — he shall be high and lifted up — as many were astonished at you, his appearance was so marred — so shall he sprinkle many nations — is the opening of the fourth and final Servant Song, which continues through Chapter 53. 1QIsa-a preserves the text clearly.

[ORIT GE'EZ] The Orit Ge'ez preserves Chapter 52 in full. Verse 7 — how beautiful upon the mountains are the feet of him who brings good news, who publishes peace, who brings good news of happiness, who publishes salvation, who says to Tziyon, Your Creator reigns — is one of the most celebrated covenant proclamation verses in the Ethiopian canonical tradition.

[MASORETIC VARIANT] 1QIsa-a and the Masoretic Text are in close agreement for Chapter 52. Verse 11-12 — depart, depart — touch no unclean thing — you shall not go out in haste — for YHWH will go before you and be your rear guard. The covenant Exodus imagery: unlike the first Exodus where Yisra'EL fled in haste — Shemot 12:11 — the new Exodus will be unhurried because YHWH leads and guards simultaneously.

[CRITICAL NOTE] Verse 13-15 — my servant shall be high and lifted up — as many were astonished at you, his appearance was so marred — so shall he sprinkle many nations. The covenant paradox of the servant's career: extreme disfigurement producing extreme exaltation, and astonishment producing understanding among kings and nations. The logical connection between the marred appearance and the sprinkling of nations is the covenant logic that suffering endured in YHWH's service produces covenant cleansing for those who witness it.

YESHA'YAHU — CHAPTER 53 He Was Despised and Rejected — Surely He Has Borne Our Griefs — He Was Wounded for Our Transgressions — The Fourth Servant Song

1 Who has believed what he has heard from us? And to whom has the arm of YHWH been revealed?

2 For he grew up before him like a young plant, and like a root out of dry ground. He had no form or majesty that we should look at him, and no beauty that we should desire him.

3 He was despised and rejected by men — a man of sorrows, and acquainted with grief. And as one from whom men hide their faces he was despised, and we esteemed him not.

4 Surely he has borne our griefs and carried our sorrows. Yet we esteemed him stricken, struck down by Creator, and afflicted.

5 But he was pierced for our transgressions — he was crushed for our iniquities. Upon him was the chastisement that brought us peace, and with his wounds we are healed.

6 All we like sheep have gone astray — we have turned — every one — to his own way. And YHWH has laid on him the iniquity of us all.

7 He was oppressed, and he was afflicted, yet he opened not his mouth. Like a lamb that is led to the slaughter, and like a sheep that before its shearers is silent, so he opened not his mouth.

8 By oppression and judgment he was taken away. And as for his generation, who considered that he was cut off out of the land of the living, stricken for the transgression of my people?

9 And they made his grave with the wicked and with a rich man in his death, although he had done no violence, and there was no deceit in his mouth.

10 Yet it was the will of YHWH to crush him — he has put him to grief. When his soul makes an offering for guilt, he shall see his offspring — he shall prolong his days. The will of YHWH shall prosper in his hand.

11 Out of the anguish of his soul he shall see and be satisfied. By his knowledge shall the righteous one, my servant, make many to be accounted righteous, and he shall bear their iniquities.

12 Therefore I will divide him a portion with the many, and he shall divide the spoil with the strong, because he poured out his soul to death and was numbered with the transgressors. Yet he bore the sin of many, and makes intercession for the transgressors.

COMPARATIVE MANUSCRIPT NOTES

[DSS] 1QIsa-a preserves Chapter 53 in full. The fourth Servant Song — Chapters 52:13-53:12 — is the covenant theological center of the second half of Yesha'yahu. 1QIsa-a shows no significant textual variants from the Masoretic in Chapter 53. The covenant portrait of the servant's vicarious suffering is preserved with precision.

[ORIT GE'EZ] The Orit Ge'ez preserves Chapter 53 in full. Verse 5 — he was pierced for our transgressions, crushed for our iniquities — upon him was the chastisement that brought us peace, and with his wounds we are healed — is read in the Ethiopian canonical tradition as the covenant description of the servant's substitutionary suffering on behalf of the covenant community.

[MASORETIC VARIANT] 1QIsa-a and the Masoretic Text are in close agreement for Chapter 53. The covenant text is one of the most carefully preserved in the entire scroll. Verse 11 — 1QIsa-a reads after the anguish of his soul he shall see light and be satisfied — adding the word or — which is absent in the Masoretic. The addition of light in 1QIsa-a is significant: the servant sees light after the anguish.

[CRITICAL NOTE] Verse 6 — YHWH has laid on him the iniquity of us all. The covenant theology of the servant's suffering is substitutionary in direction and scope: the iniquity of all is directed onto the one servant. Verse 10 — yet it was the will of YHWH to crush him. The covenant paradox: the suffering is neither accidental nor unjust in the divine ordering — it is the will of YHWH. The covenant suffering of the servant produces offspring — verse 10 — and prolonged days — and the satisfaction of seeing many made righteous — verse 11. The servant who was numbered with transgressors — verse 12 — intercedes for them. The covenant circle is complete: the one who bore the iniquity also intercedes for those whose iniquity he bore.

YESHA'YAHU — CHAPTER 54 Sing O Barren One — The Mountains May Depart But My Steadfast Love Shall Not — The Heritage of the Servants of YHWH

1 Sing, O barren one, who did not bear — break forth into singing and cry aloud, you who have not been in labor. For the children of the desolate one will be more than the children of her who is married, says YHWH.

2 Enlarge the place of your tent, and let the curtains of your habitations be stretched out. Do not hold back — lengthen your cords and strengthen your stakes.

3 For you will spread abroad to the right and to the left, and your offspring will possess the nations and will people the desolate cities.

4 Fear not, for you will not be ashamed. Be not confounded, for you will not be disgraced. For you will forget the shame of your youth, and the reproach of your widowhood you will remember no more.

5 For your Maker is your husband — YHWH of hosts is his name — and the Holy One of Yisra'EL is your Redeemer, the Creator of the whole earth he is called.

6 For YHWH has called you like a wife deserted and grieved in spirit, like a wife of youth when she is cast off, says your Creator.

7 For a brief moment I deserted you, but with great compassion I will gather you.

8 In overflowing anger for a moment I hid my face from you, but with everlasting steadfast love I will have compassion on you, says YHWH, your Redeemer.

9 This is like the days of Noakh to me: as I swore that the waters of Noakh should no more go over the earth, so I have sworn that I will not be angry with you and will not rebuke you.

10 For the mountains may depart and the hills be removed, but my steadfast love shall not depart from you, and my covenant of peace shall not be removed, says YHWH, who has compassion on you.

11 O afflicted one, storm-tossed and not comforted, behold, I will set your stones in antimony, and lay your foundations with sapphires.

12 I will make your pinnacles of agate, your gates of carbuncles, and all your wall of precious stones.

13 All your children shall be taught by YHWH, and great shall be the peace of your children.

14 In righteousness you shall be established. You shall be far from oppression, for you shall not fear. And from terror, for it shall not come near you.

15 If anyone stirs up strife, it is not from me. Whoever stirs up strife with you shall fall because of you.

16 Behold, I have created the smith who blows the fire of coals and produces a weapon for its purpose. I have also created the ravager to destroy.

17 No weapon that is fashioned against you shall succeed, and you shall refute every tongue that rises against you in judgment. This is the heritage of the servants of YHWH and their vindication from me, declares YHWH.

COMPARATIVE MANUSCRIPT NOTES

[DSS] 1QIsa-a preserves Chapter 54 in full. Chapter 54 follows the suffering of Chapter 53 with the covenant promise of restoration to the desolate wife: Tziyon, widowed and barren through the exile, is called to enlarge her tent for the children who will return. The covenant restoration exceeds the covenant loss.

[ORIT GE'EZ] The Orit Ge'ez preserves Chapter 54 in full. Verse 10 — the mountains may depart and the hills be removed, but my steadfast love shall not depart from you, and my covenant of peace shall not be removed — is read as the covenant guarantee that outlasts creation itself: the physical mountains are less permanent than YHWH's covenant faithfulness.

[MASORETIC VARIANT] 1QIsa-a and the Masoretic Text are in close agreement for Chapter 54. Verse 9 — this is like the days of Noakh to me — as I swore that the waters of Noakh should no more go over the earth, so I have sworn I will not be angry with you. The Noakh covenant becomes the covenant precedent for the new covenant with the restored Tziyon: unconditional, sworn, permanent.

[CRITICAL NOTE] Verse 7-8 — for a brief moment I deserted you — but with great compassion I will gather you. In overflowing anger for a moment I hid my face — but with everlasting steadfast love I will have compassion. The covenant proportion: a moment of desertion against everlasting steadfast love — brief hiding of the face against great compassion. The exile is real — but it is covenant brevity measured against covenant eternity. Verse 17 — no weapon formed against you shall succeed. The covenant declaration closes Chapter 54 with the covenant heritage: the servants of YHWH inherit covenant protection — every weapon against them fails, every accusation against them is refuted.

END OF PART FOUR — CHAPTERS 42 THROUGH 54
